mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher after 3-month FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises sharply, markets infer weakening household income and profit growth, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 20 trading days. Small unemployment changes are not enough; the signal requires a clear labor deterioration shock.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce below-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if positive 20d returns occur in more than 60% of trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad final-demand strength and a healthy consumer housing backdrop, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The joint confirmation matters more than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-and-HOUST signal fails to outperform either proxy alone, or if 60d forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of high-signal cases.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while CPIAUCSL stays contained, firms are expanding real output without a binding inflation shock, which supports margin stability and positive near-term equity returns. The signal fails if industrial gains coincide with rising inflation pressure.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation is below 2% annualized
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO shocks combined with low CPI do not lead to above-baseline 20d returns, or if the signal flips negative when CPI inflation is contained.

mechanism_narrative: A Fed funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive policy that compresses risk appetite and forward earnings multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is strongest when the policy rate is still rising.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate episodes do not show lower 5d SPX returns than lower-rate episodes, or if the sign is positive in more than 55% of high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth together with falling unemployment indicates broad labor-market strength, which supports consumption and earnings expectations and should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is weaker if payroll gains are offset by unemployment deterioration.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal does not outperform either series alone, or if 20d SPX returns are not positive after synchronized labor improvement.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth combined with flat or declining retail sales signals demand deceleration that should be priced as a profit slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. This is a macro slowdown signal, not a market-timing reversal.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth is below 1.5% annualized and RSAFS monthly growth is non-positive
failure_condition: Reject if slowdown-triggered observations do not produce below-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate despite concurrent weak GDP and sales.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts with rising industrial production marks an inventory and construction upswing that usually leads broader cyclical equities higher over the next month. The mechanism breaks when housing strength is isolated and production is flat.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month growth is positive and INDPRO 3-month growth is positive
failure_condition: Reject if joint housing-production upswings fail to yield higher 20d SPX returns than baseline, or if the effect disappears when one proxy is positive and the other is flat.
